prompt:
Need an async memoizer for our data-access layer (memo.ts, export memoizeAsync). My first instinct was to JSON.stringify the argument as a Map key, but half our loaders take live model objects with cycles and getters, so that's out — key on the argument itself: object/function arguments go in a WeakMap so cache entries can be collected with the object, primitives in a regular Map (mind NaN — it should still hit). Optional { key: (arg) => k } option to derive the key instead (we often want user.id, not the user object); whatever it returns gets the same WeakMap/Map treatment.

Behavior I need, in order of how much the current ad-hoc caches get it wrong:
1. If a call for a key is already in flight, join it — fn runs once, everyone gets the same promise. (Today we fire three identical Mongo queries per page load.)
2. Rejections propagate to every waiter but are NEVER cached; the next call retries fn.
3. Resolved values cached indefinitely until evicted: m.delete(arg) -> boolean, m.clear().
4. The nasty one: clear()/delete() while a load is in flight. In-flight callers still get their (now stale) answer, but that answer must not be written back over whatever loads afterwards.

fn takes exactly one argument. Contract = test_memo.ts, `node --test test_memo.ts`, tests are read-only as far as you're concerned. Plain TS, node built-ins only.
